description_wiki: Humboldt Park is one of the 77 community areas of Chicago in Illinois, United States, located on the West Side of Chicago.

description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description_wiki: Ireland is an island in the North Atlantic Ocean, in Northwestern Europe.

description: US federal government agency that maintains information on those potentially subject to military conscription
description_wiki: The Selective Service System (SSS) is an independent agency of the United States government that maintains a database of registered male U.S. citizens and other U.S. residents potentially subject to military conscription (i.e., the draft).

description: census-designated place in Pulaski County, Missouri, United States
description_wiki: Fort Leonard Wood is a U.S. Army training installation located in the Missouri Ozarks.

description: high school athletic conference in Illinois
description_wiki: The Chicago Catholic League (CCL) is a high school athletic conference based in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 25th Infantry Division (nicknamed "Tropic Lightning") is a United States Army division based at Schofield Barracks in Hawaii.

description: city and county seat of Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Oakland is a city in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area in the U.S. state of California.

description: airport in Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Chicago O'Hare International Airport (IATA: ORD, ICAO: KORD, FAA LID: ORD) is the primary international airport serving Chicago, Illinois, United States, located on the city's Northwest Side, approximately 17 miles (27 km) northwest of downtown.

description: US Air Force base near Fairfield, California, United States
description_wiki: Travis Air Force Base (IATA: SUU, ICAO: KSUU, FAA LID: SUU) is a United States Air Force base under the operational control of Air Mobility Command (AMC), located three miles (5 km) east of the central business district of the city of Fairfield, in Solano County, California.

aliases: The City of Lights and Flowers
description: consolidated city-borough, and largest city in Alaska, United States of America
description_wiki: Anchorage (Denaʼina: Dgheyay Kaq'; Dgheyaytnu), officially the Municipality of Anchorage, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Alaska.

description: city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokosuka (横須賀市, Yokosuka-shi; pronounced [jokosɯ̥ka]) is a city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan.

description: largest city in Vietnam
description_wiki: Ho Chi Minh City (HCMC; Vietnamese: Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, IPA: [tʰaɲ˨˩ fow˦˥ ho˨˩ cɪj˦˥ mɨn˧˧] ), also known as Saigon, is the most populous municipality of Vietnam, with a population of more than 14 million in 2025.

description: rural district of Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
description_wiki: Củ Chi is a rural district of Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.

description: former state in in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: South Vietnam, officially the Republic of Vietnam (RVN; Vietnamese: Việt Nam Cộng hòa, VNCH), was a country in Southeast Asia that existed from 1955 to 1975.

description: country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: Cambodia, officially the Kingdom of Cambodia, is a country in Mainland Southeast Asia.

entity: Army of the Republic of Vietnam
description: Ground Forces branch of the Republic of Vietnam’s military Forces
description_wiki: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam (ARVN; Vietnamese: Lục quân Việt Nam Cộng hòa; French: Armée de la république du Viêt Nam) composed the ground forces of the South Vietnamese military from its inception in 1955 to the fall of Saigon on 30 April 1975.
